name: Alpaca Trading API
what_it_does: Alpaca is a commission-free stock and cryptocurrency trading API designed for developers and algorithmic traders. It supports market, limit, and stop orders, portfolio management, real-time market data streaming via WebSocket, and paper trading for strategy testing without real money.
use_cases: Building algorithmic trading bots and automated trading strategies
Paper trading for testing trading agents before going live
Real-time market data ingestion and analysis
Fractional share trading for micro-investment apps
not_for: Institutional or high-frequency trading (not built for sub-millisecond execution)
International users outside the US (primarily US-based brokerage)
Trading futures, forex, or commodities (equities and crypto only)
best_when: An agent needs to execute US equity or crypto trades programmatically, especially for algorithmic strategies or automated portfolio management.
avoid_when: Sub-millisecond execution latency is required, institutional-grade clearing is needed, or trading instruments beyond US stocks and crypto.

auth.notes: API Key ID and API Secret Key pair, generated in the Alpaca dashboard. Separate key pairs for paper and live trading environments. Keys are per-account. No OAuth or fine-grained scopes — a key grants full account access. OAuth2 available for broker-dealer API (multi-account).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Paper trading is fully free and unlimited. Live trading is commission-free for US equities.
pricing.paid_tiers: Crypto trading fees apply (0.1-0.25% per trade). Market data subscriptions: unlimited plan ~$9/mo for real-time data (free tier has 15-min delay).
pricing.notes: The trading API itself has no subscription fee. Revenue comes from payment for order flow, crypto spreads, and premium market data. Paper trading is an excellent free sandbox for agent development.

performance.rate_limits: 200 requests/minute for trading endpoints. Market data has separate higher limits. WebSocket streaming has no request limits.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors returned as JSON with code, message fields. HTTP status codes are used correctly. Order rejection reasons are specific and actionable (insufficient_funds, invalid_order_type, market_closed, etc.). WebSocket error events include descriptive messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: client_order_id can be specified on order creation for idempotency — if an order with the same client_order_id already exists, the existing order is returned. Strongly recommended for agents to set this on every order.
known_agent_gotchas: Paper and live trading environments use different base URLs and key pairs — easy to accidentally use live keys in testing
Market hours matter — orders submitted outside market hours behave differently (held, rejected, or extended-hours depending on flags)
Order fills are asynchronous — agents must poll order status or stream updates via WebSocket
Pattern Day Trader (PDT) rule applies — less than $25k account triggers restrictions on day trades
Fractional shares cannot be used with all order types
Crypto trading fees apply unlike equity trading — agents tracking P&L must account for spread
Free market data tier has 15-minute delay — real-time data requires a paid subscription
Rate limits (200 req/min) are easy to hit in tight agent loops — implement backoff

score_rationale: Alpaca is one of the most agent-friendly trading APIs available: commission-free, developer-first design, free paper trading environment, client_order_id for idempotency, and a Python SDK that is actively maintained. The main friction points are asynchronous order fills requiring polling or WebSocket integration, market hours awareness, and PDT rules for smaller accounts. The clear separation of paper and live environments is excellent for agent development safety.

security_score_components.security_notes: API key + secret key pair. Paper trading sandbox. FINRA-regulated broker-dealer. SOC2. CRITICAL: Production API executes real trades. Always use paper trading until thoroughly tested and validated.
